titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Italy ( Dunn ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Italy ( Dunn ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
1852. Inform Treasury for Tasca. Several meetings have been held regarding amendment to Corbino AgreementSee Foreign Relations, 1946, vol. v, p. 932. for purchase of surplus property. Apparently a stumbling block is question of valuation of lend-lease material turned over by UK to Italy under April 17 Anglo-Italo financial agreement. (Embassy’s despatch 609, April 28, 1947, page 20, of attached agreementNot printed.) UK apparently stated to Italians at time amount involved did not exceed one million pounds, whereas Italians subsequently discovered 13 to 14 million pounds of US material involved. See Embassy’s despatch 988, June 13,Not printed. containing procès-verbal signed in May. Italian Government now reluctant to assume dollar burden involved in such debt. Italians also consider such items as airfields, fixed installations and bridges overpriced. Minister Del Vecchio stated in this connection that he considered the basic Corbino-Bonner
Surplus Property Agreement unfavorable to Italy, particularly when compared with the Anglo-Italian Agreement for the disposition of surplus property in Italy.

Although Del Vecchio had expressly wished negotiations conducted without the presence of other Ministers, under insistence of Einaudi, a meeting is to be held as soon as possible with the Director General of Economic Affairs of Foreign Office and Del Vecchio, primarily because of the lend-lease item.
